entities.name: CAJANUS PLATYCARPUS
entities.description: A wild relative of pigeon pea used for evaluating agronomic traits and crossability.

entities.name: PIGEON PEA
entities.description: The cultivated species Cajanus cajan used as a comparison and for crossing experiments.

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENTS
entities.description: Various measurements contributing to the overall yield of the plant.

entities.name: SEED PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of protein present in the seeds, measured as a percentage.

entities.name: ICRISAT ASIA CENTER
entities.description: The research facility where the Cajanus platycarpus accessions were grown.

entities.name: NUMBER OF PRIMARY BRANCHES
entities.description: The count of main branches on the plant, positively associated with seed yield.

entities.name: POD LENGTH
entities.description: The length of the seed pods.

entities.name: POD WIDTH
entities.description: The width of the seed pods.

entities.name: BRANCHING
entities.description: The pattern and extent of branch development on the plant.

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the seeds.

entities.name: DAYS TO FLOWER
entities.description: The duration from planting to the onset of flowering.

entities.name: DAYS TO MATURE
entities.description: The duration from planting to physiological maturity.

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The total weight of seeds produced per plant.

entities.name: PODS PER PLANT
entities.description: The total number of pods produced per plant, positively associated with seed yield.

entities.name: METHIONINE
entities.description: An essential amino acid found in high amounts in certain accessions of C. platycarpus.

entities.name: CYSTEINE
entities.description: An amino acid found in high amounts in certain accessions of C. platycarpus.

entities.name: CROSSING
entities.description: The process of pollinating one plant with the pollen of another to produce hybrids.

entities.name: CROSSABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of two different plant lines or species to produce fertile offspring.
